seoTitle: Clarence vs Clarence Town — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Clarence and Clarence Town. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Clarence vs Clarence Town: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Clarence and Clarence Town.
heading: Clarence vs Clarence Town — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Clarence, New South Wales and Clarence Town, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Clarence has a lower overall climate risk score (35/100) compared to Clarence Town (43/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Clarence or Clarence Town safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Clarence has a flood risk score of 36/100, while Clarence Town scores 44/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Clarence scores 37/100 for bushfire risk. Clarence Town scores 64/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Clarence or Clarence Town?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Clarence has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.
